job_description:
Sr. Visual Designer - 2Base Technologies

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process. The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

What You Will Own

  • The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline
  • Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences
  • Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking
  • On-page SEO across all published pages
  • Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data
  • Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification
  • The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages
  • SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing
  • Link building target approval. The outreach team identifies candidates, and you approve or reject based on defined quality standards
  • SEO performance reporting, including organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, and Search Console health

What You Will Not Own

  • Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages
  • Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard
  • Paid search: that is a separate role
  • Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner
  • Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements

  • Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role, with strong candidates typically at the 4 to 6 year mark
  • Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content
  • Experience managing technical SEO independently
  • Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture
  • Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool
  • Experience working across US and/or UK markets
  • Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO
  • Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without needing clarification

Mindset

  • Thinks in systems, not tasks
  • Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments
  • Analytically honest
  • Collaborative but not passive

What We Are Not Looking For

  • SEO practitioners whose entire experience is informational content
  • Candidates who treat Search Console as a monthly reporting task
  • Candidates who cannot explain the difference between high-intent and informational keyword targeting
  • Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on tools
  • Candidates with no exposure to US or UK search behaviour

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Strategic SEO leader to build a scalable organic search system for US/UK markets, focusing on high-intent content architecture and technical execution.

job_description:
Sr. Business Analyst

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role. It is not a screen updater role. It is not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modelling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

As a Senior Business Analyst, you will be the functional brain of the project. You will ensure that the right problem is understood, broken down, analyzed, validated, clarified, communicated, and owned end-to-end. You will drive requirement precision, reduce rework, and enable confident and aligned delivery across teams.

Key Responsibilities - Lead requirement workshops, client calls, and discovery discussions to understand business needs clearly. - Ask deeper questions, challenge assumptions, and validate the true business intent behind every requirement. - Convert ambiguous client inputs into structured, logical, and actionable functional understanding. - Break large features into modules, workflows, components, and clear dependencies. - Build strong system-level understanding involving APIs, integrations, data flow, and backend considerations. - Translate business processes into functional logic that development teams can execute. - Decompose requirements into use-case flows, system behaviours, edge cases, alternate paths, validation rules, and dependencies. - Prepare high-quality documentation, including BRD, FRD, SRS, workflows, state diagrams, data-flow diagrams, API requirement specs, and acceptance criteria. - Ensure all documentation is clear, logical, structured, testable, and ready for developers and QA to use. - Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product owners to validate feasibility and align understanding. - Ensure QA teams can derive test cases effectively from your functional logic and acceptance criteria. - Communicate confidently with clients, handle requirement discussions smoothly, and clarify misunderstandings immediately. - Push back politely on unclear or changing requirements and ensure final decisions are documented properly. - Maintain requirement traceability throughout the lifecycle and ensure zero ambiguity during handover to development. - Own the entire requirement lifecycle from discovery to development readiness with high accountability. - Drive clarity across teams by ensuring every requirement is complete, validated, and logically consistent.

Requirements - Required Skills & Experience - Minimum 4+ years of core Business Analyst experience with strong analytical and system-level thinking. - Ability to break down complex requirements into clear components, workflows, and functional logic. - Excellent verbal and written communication with confidence in client interactions and clarifications. - Proven experience in preparing SRS, BRD, FRD, workflows, and acceptance criteria with high clarity. - Ability to challenge assumptions politely, ask the right questions, and ensure zero ambiguity. - Strong collaboration experience with development, QA, architects, and cross-functional teams. - Understanding of API behaviours, integrations, data flows, and multi-module system interactions. - Ability to run requirement discussions, workshops, and independently lead discovery sessions. - Exposure to SaaS, scalable products, automation, or AI-supported systems is an added advantage. - Demonstrated ownership of requirements end-to-end, ensuring developer-ready and testable outputs. - Not suitable for UI-only thinkers, passive communicators, template-based writers, or those relying on AI for answers. - Success is defined by clear, testable requirements, zero rework, strong cross-team alignment, and confident communication. - Minimum Qualifications - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or related field - 4 to 8 years of experience as a Business Analyst or Product Analyst - Experience in SaaS or scalable product environments - Exposure to AI/Automation, integrations, or multi-module systems - Ability to run independent client workshops - Must have independently handled requirement ownership for at least one full project lifecycle

Benefits - A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.

Job Summary

The Compliance Officer plays a critical role in safeguarding the integrity of client entities by ensuring that their operations adhere to all relevant legal, regulatory, and statutory requirements. The primary responsibility is to support and oversee the compliance framework for the allotted entities, ensuring that filings and submissions to government authorities are accurate, timely, and aligned with regulatory expectations. This role involves identifying applicable laws, monitoring potential areas of non-adherence, and assisting in the implementation of effective strategies to ensure compliance across assigned entities. The Compliance Officer will also help foster a culture of responsibility by maintaining policies, conducting basic risk reviews, and coordinating with internal teams and external consultants to promote consistent regulatory adherence.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Regulatory Adherence – Ensure the organization’s operations for allotted entities fully comply with applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies
  • Statutory Filings & Submissions – Oversee accuracy and timeliness of all filings and submissions to regulatory authorities
  • Policy Implementation & Monitoring – Develop, implement, and enforce compliance procedures and policies; ensure business units operate within legal and regulatory frameworks
  • Risk Assessment & Management – Conduct regular risk assessments to identify potential areas of noncompliance; recommend and monitor preventive and corrective actions
  • Reporting & Monitoring – Maintain compliance records and reporting systems; provide regular updates and performance reports
  • Investigation & Remediation – Investigate potential compliance breaches, implement corrective measures, and ensure preventive actions are in place
  • Management Assurance – Provide periodic assurance to senior management on the overall compliance status, highlighting key risks, corrective actions taken, and areas requiring attention

Secondary Responsibilities

  • Collaboration with Departments – Coordinate with internal teams and external consultants to ensure compliance adherence across all business operations
  • Training & Awareness – Facilitate training sessions and awareness programs for employees regarding regulatory requirements and internal policies
  • Monitoring Regulatory Changes – Track updates in laws and regulations; ensure timely amendments to policies and procedures
  • Audit Support – Assist in internal and external audits, ensuring compliance issues are addressed and audit recommendations implemented
  • Continuous Improvement – Identify trends, recurring issues, or process gaps; recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce risk exposure

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Accounting, or a related field with a professional certification of Company Secretary mandatory
